segments.text: Contend, O LORD, with those who contend with me; fight against those who fight against me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Take hold of shield and buckler and rise for my help.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Draw the spear and javelin against my pursuers; say to my soul, 'I am your salvation.'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them be put to shame and dishonor who seek my life; let them be turned back and disappointed who devise evil against me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them be like chaff before the wind, with the angel of the LORD driving them away.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let their way be dark and slippery, with the angel of the LORD pursuing them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For without cause they hid their net for me; without cause they dug a pit for my life.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let destruction come upon him when he does not know it, and let the net that he hid catch himself; let him fall into it, to his own destruction.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: at…: Heb. which he knoweth not of

segments.text: Then my soul will rejoice in the LORD, exulting in his salvation.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: All my bones shall say,
O LORD, who is like you, delivering the poor from him who is too strong for him, the poor and needy from him who robs him?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Malicious witnesses rise up; they ask me of things that I do not know.
They repay me evil for good; my soul is bereft.
But I, when they were sick— I wore sackcloth; I afflicted myself with fasting; I prayed with head bowed on my chest.
I went about as though he were my friend or brother; as one who mourns his mother, I bowed down in grief.

footnotes: as though…: Heb. as a friend, as a brother to me

segments.text: But at my stumbling they rejoiced and gathered together; they gathered together against me; wretches whom I did not know tore me apart without ceasing;
like profane mockers at a feast, they gnash their teeth at me.
How long, O Lord, will you look on? Rescue me from their destruction, my precious life from the lions!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will thank you in the great congregation; in the mighty throng I will praise you.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let not those rejoice over me who are wrongfully my foes, and let not those wink the eye who hate me without cause.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For they do not speak peace, but against those who are quiet in the land they devise words of deceit.
They open wide their mouths against me; they say,
Aha, Aha! Our eyes have seen it!
segments.speaker: David's Enemies

segments.text: You have seen, O LORD; be not silent! O Lord, be not far from me!
Awake and rouse yourself for my vindication, for my cause, my God and my Lord!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Vindicate me according to your righteousness, O LORD my God; and let them not rejoice over me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them not say in their hearts, 'Aha, our desire!' Let them not say, 'We have swallowed him up.'
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: Ah…: Heb. Ah, ah, our soul

segments.text: Let those be put to shame and disappointed altogether who rejoice at my calamity; let those be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves against me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let those who delight in my righteousness shout for joy and be glad and say evermore, 'Great is the LORD, who delights in the welfare of his servant!'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then my tongue shall tell of your righteousness and of your praise all the day long.
segments.speaker: David
